TechCrunch.title: Florida AG to probe OpenAI, alleging possible connection to FSU shooting
TechCrunch.summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier plans to investigate OpenAI for its alleged harm to minors, potential to threaten national security, and its possible link to a shooting at Florida State University last year.

TechCrunch.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
TechCrunch.summary: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

TechCrunch.title: EFF is the latest organization to leave X
TechCrunch.summary: The EFF follows the departure of various news organizations and others who no longer find X a viable source of traffic.

TechCrunch.title: What founders can learn from Anjuna’s layoffs and recovery
TechCrunch.summary: In 2021, Anjuna Security was growing fast, hiring aggressively, and chasing a market that seemed limitless. TechCrunch.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
TechCrunch.summary: ChatGPT had reportedly been used to plan the attack that killed two and injured five at Florida State University last April. The family of one victim has said that they plan to sue OpenAI over the incident.

TechCrunch.title: StubHub to pay $10M to settle FTC allegations over ‘deceptive’ ticket pricing
TechCrunch.summary: The consumer protection agency says the company violated the FTC Act and its Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees by "deceptively" advertising ticket prices on its website without clearly disclosing upfront what the total cost would be, including all mandatory fees.

TechCrunch.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
TechCrunch.summary: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world. Are real cybersecurity concerns a cover for a bigger problem at the frontier lab?
